item.title: - Venerable Sir, for what purpose is right contemplation?" - "Right contemplation, Rādha, has disenchantment as its purpose." - "Venerable Sir, what is the purpose…
item.description:
- Venerable Sir, for what purpose is right contemplation?" - "Right contemplation, Rādha, has disenchantment as its purpose." - "Venerable Sir, what is the purpose of disenchantment?" - "Disenchantment, Rādha, has dispassion as its purpose." - "Venerable Sir, what is the purpose of dispassion?" - "Dispassion, Rādha, has liberation as its purpose." - "Venerable Sir, what is the purpose of liberation?" - "Liberation, Rādha, has Nibbāna as its purpose." - "Venerable Sir, what is the purpose of Nibbāna?" - "Rādha, your question goes too far; you cannot grasp the limit of the question. The holy life is grounded in Nibbāna, culminates in Nibbāna, ends in Nibbāna.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 23.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, when walking for alms in the village or town, receives coarse or fine food as much as he wishes. He thinks:…
item.description:
Furthermore, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, when walking for alms in the village or town, receives coarse or fine food as much as he wishes. He thinks: 'I walked for alms in the village or town and received coarse or fine food as much as I wished. Now my body is heavy, cannot bear it, heavy like a bean soaked in water. Let me lie down.' He lies down, not making an effort... unrealized. Bhikkhus, this is the sixth ground for laziness.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 8.80
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Nun Uttarā: People in their youth, Holding pestles, pounding rice, Youths gain wealth, Supporting their wives and children. Follow the Buddha's teaching, Do nothing…
item.description:
Elder Nun Uttarā:

People in their youth,
Holding pestles, pounding rice,
Youths gain wealth,
Supporting their wives and children.

Follow the Buddha's teaching,
Do nothing that causes distress.
Wash your feet quickly,
Sit down to one side.

Having established the mind,
One-pointed, well concentrated,
Contemplate conditioned things,
External, not belonging to self.

I heard the instruction,
The words of Paṭācārā.
After washing my feet,
I entered and sat down to one side.

In the first watch of the night,
I remembered my past lives.
In the middle watch,
My divine eye was purified.

In the last watch,
I shattered the mass of delusion.
From the three knowledges, rising,
The instruction is done.

Like the lord Sakka
In the Thirty-three heaven,
Never defeated
On any battlefield,
I live, honoring you
As the teacher and leader.
I have attained the three knowledges,
I am without taints.

From: Therīgāthā 7.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: One who here has abandoned sensual desires, Who has gone forth from home to homelessness, Who has destroyed sensual existence — Him I call a…
item.description:
One who here has abandoned sensual desires,
Who has gone forth from home to homelessness,
Who has destroyed sensual existence —
Him I call a brahmin.

One who here has abandoned craving,
Who has gone forth from home to homelessness,
Who has destroyed craving for existence —
Him I call a brahmin.

Having left behind human bonds,
Having transcended celestial bonds,
Freed from all bonds —
Him I call a brahmin.

Having left behind delight and aversion,
Cooled, without attachments,
The hero who has conquered all worlds,
Who has subdued all —
Him I call a brahmin.

From: Dhammapada 383-423
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Here, Nāgita, when I see bhikkhus who, after eating until satisfied, until their bellies are full, dwell devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the…
item.description:
Here, Nāgita, when I see bhikkhus who, after eating until satisfied, until their bellies are full, dwell devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of touch, the pleasure of sleeping, then, Nāgita, I think: 'Indeed, these venerable ones find with difficulty, with trouble, with effort, the bliss of security, the bliss of seclusion, the bliss of peace, the bliss of enlightenment; while I attain without difficulty, without trouble, without effort, the bliss of security, the bliss of seclusion, the bliss of peace, the bliss of enlightenment — because these venerable ones, after eating until satisfied, until their bellies are full, dwell devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of touch, the pleasure of sleeping.'

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 8.86
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, bhikkhus, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving, this body of the wise person has originated. That ignorance, the wise person abandons. That craving,…
item.description:
And, bhikkhus, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving, this body of the wise person has originated. That ignorance, the wise person abandons. That craving, the wise person destroys. Why is that? Bhikkhus, the wise person lives the holy life for the complete destruction of suffering. Therefore, the wise person, with the breakup of the body after death, does not go to another body. Not going to another body, he is freed from birth, aging, death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair. I say: 'He is freed from suffering.'

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 12.19
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Therefore, Moggallāna, you should train thus: 'I will not speak provoking words.' Thus, Moggallāna, you should train. When there are provoking words, Moggallāna, then much…
item.description:
Therefore, Moggallāna, you should train thus: 'I will not speak provoking words.' Thus, Moggallāna, you should train. When there are provoking words, Moggallāna, then much back-and-forth speech is to be expected. When there is much back-and-forth speech, then there is agitation. For one who is agitated, there is no sense restraint. For one without sense restraint, the mind is far from concentration. Moggallāna, I do not praise all kinds of association, but neither do I refrain from praising all kinds of association. Truly, Moggallāna, I do not praise association with laypeople for those gone forth. But association with dwellings that are quiet, free from noise, free from the breath of crowds, solitary, remote from people, suitable for meditative seclusion — I praise association with such dwellings.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 7.61
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus do not have evil friends, evil companions, evil associates, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.
item.description:
Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus do not have evil friends, evil companions, evil associates, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 16 – Mahāparinibbānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: The wise, abandoning dark states, Develop bright states, Leaving home for homelessness, In seclusion, hard to delight in. Seek delight in Nibbāna, Abandon sensual pleasures,…
item.description:
The wise, abandoning dark states,
Develop bright states,
Leaving home for homelessness,
In seclusion, hard to delight in.

Seek delight in Nibbāna,
Abandon sensual pleasures, have nothing,
The wise cleanse themselves
Of defilements from within.

Those with right mind,
Well develop the factors of enlightenment,
Abandon all attachments,
Rejoicing, not clinging,
Without taints, radiant,
They live at peace in the world.

From: Dhammapada 76-89
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Bhūta: When the wise person sees that old age and death are suffering, The ordinary person, not seeing, grasps suffering. Having fully understood suffering,…
item.description:
Elder Bhūta:

When the wise person sees that old age and death are suffering,
The ordinary person, not seeing, grasps suffering.
Having fully understood suffering, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When craving is destroyed — craving that brings suffering and poison,
Creates proliferation and bondage, pushes toward suffering —
Having destroyed craving, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When with wisdom one sees the supreme good path,
Consisting of eight parts in two groups, purifying all defilements,
Having seen with wisdom, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When one develops the path — sorrowless, stainless,
Unconditioned, peaceful path, purifying all defilements,
Cutting the bonds of fetters, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When thundercloud drums roar in the sky,
Along the bird's path, a dense rain pours down,
A bhikkhu going to a cave, developing, sits in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When on the riverbanks, the forest garlands bloom and shine
With many colors and hues,
With a relaxed mind, sitting in meditation on the riverbank,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When in the dead of night, the forest silent,
A torrential rain pours down,
Tusked beasts are roaring as they live,
A bhikkhu going to a mountainside sits silently in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When applied and sustained thought are subdued,
In a mountain cave, sitting in meditation at ease,
Without fear, without obstruction,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When sitting with joy and bliss, without defilement, without sorrow,
Unconfined, free from craving, not struck by the dart,
All taints ended, that one sits in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

From: Theragāthā 9.1
📖 Read this sutta
